steps.title: Use the shared angle
steps.content: The triangles already have one pair of congruent angles: angle $A$ and angle $P$ each measure $62$ degrees. One more matching angle pair is enough to prove similarity by angle-angle similarity.

steps.title: Test the angle information
steps.content:
If angle $B$ measures $40$ degrees and angle $R$ measures $78$ degrees, then the missing angles are
$$C=180-62-40=78$$
$$Q=180-62-78=40$$

steps.title: Match the corresponding angles
steps.content:
The angle pairs match as $A$ to $P$, $B$ to $Q$, and $C$ to $R$:
$$62,40,78$$
This is sufficient to prove the triangles similar. The answer is C.
